note: RWAMOD RW_2025 active-instrument map to governing Rwanda law. Instrument keys are the model's EUROMOD-convention policy names (structure metadata only; no licensed content). Governing-law attributions are working hypotheses until the print hunt confirms them; source URLs are hunting grounds, not captured provenance - capture happens in axiom-corpus manifests.

instruments.instrument: tin_rw (PAYE employment income tax, three bases)
instruments.governing_law: Law no 027/2022 of 20/10/2022 establishing taxes on income, employment-income monthly schedule as amended (the amendment fixing the CY2024+ bands 0/60,000/100,000/200,000 FRW at 0/10/20/30%); casual-labour rate in the same article

Law no 027/2022 lump-sum regime: flat amounts by annual-turnover band (60,000/120,000/210,000/300,000 FRW for 2m-12m) and 3% of turnover for 12m-20m; agriculture exemption to 12m - flat amounts possibly fixed by Ministerial Order under the law
Decentralized-entities revenue law (rental income tax: taxable base = 50% of gross rental income; annual bands 0/180,001/1,000,001 FRW at 0/20/30%)
Pension scheme law and the 2024/2025 contribution-rate instrument raising the mandatory pension contribution to 6% employee + 6% employer from January 2025
Occupational hazards branch: employer 2% of covered earnings under the RSSB framework
Law no 003/2016 of 30/03/2016 establishing and governing the maternity leave benefits scheme: 0.3% employee + 0.3% employer

instruments.instrument: tsceehl_rw / tscerhl_rw / tscpehl_rw (medical schemes: RAMA, CBHI, MMI)
instruments.governing_law: RAMA medical scheme 7.5% employee + 7.5% employer (+7.5% pensioner); CBHI tiered annual premiums by monthly-income tier (3,000/5,000/8,000/20,000 FRW) plus the 0.5% formal-sector CBHI levy, fixed by Order under the CBHI law; MMI 5% member + 17.5% state
Law no 049/2023 of 05/09/2023 on value added tax: 18% standard rate

Excise duty law as amended: ad-valorem and specific rows (cigarettes 36% + 230 FRW/pack, wines 40/70%, spirits 60/70%, beers 40/65%, soft drinks 39%, juices 10%, water 10%, fuels 183/150 FRW per litre, lubricants 37%, vehicles 5/10/15%, airtime and telecom 12%, chocolate 1,930 FRW/kg, chewing gum 322 FRW/kg, e-cigarettes 30,000 FRW/unit + cartridges 24,400 FRW/unit, cigars 160%, cosmetics 15%)
Administrative: LODA/MINALOC Vision 2020 Umurenge Programme (VUP) Direct Support - monthly transfer scaled by household size (7,500/12,000/15,000/18,000/21,000 FRW for 1-5+ members)
Administrative: MINEDUC National School Feeding Programme per-student amounts
Administrative: petroleum pump-price stabilization (MININFRA/RURA pricing instruments)
Administrative: MINAGRI Girinka (One Cow per Poor Family) programme - in-kind cow value amortized monthly in the model
